task_completion.description: Open the Bridge Command launcher application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bridge Command launcher window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the settings or configuration menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The settings editor interface or configuration menu within the Bridge Command launcher is open.

task_completion.description: Locate the 'view_angle' or 'Horizontal Field of View' setting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'view_angle' or 'Horizontal Field of View' parameter is visible in the Graphics section of the settings editor.
The input field for 'view_angle' or 'Horizontal Field of View' explicitly shows the number '60' entered by the agent.
The agent clicks a 'Save', 'Apply', or 'OK' button in the settings editor, or the settings window closes after the value was entered, indicating the changes were saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Bridge Command GUI settings editor rather than terminal commands to modify the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Bridge Command graphical interface, not a terminal window using commands like 'sed' or 'echo' to edit bc5.ini.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Bridge Command software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Bridge Command application window is clearly visible and active, rather than a generic text editor modifying the configuration file directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by writing the target state into a generic text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no open text editors (like gedit or nano) simply containing the text 'view_angle=60' or expected state metadata.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Bridge Command is a ship bridge simulator. The 'view_angle' parameter corresponds to the 'Horizontal Field of View' setting found in the [Graphics] section of the configuration (bc5.ini). The task requires changing this value from its default of 90 to 60 using the Bridge Command settings editor GUI. The VLM should look for the 'Horizontal Field of View' or 'view_angle' field in the settings UI being updated to 60.
